summary: PR #482 lowered the DB connection pool max_size from 50 to 10, exhausting the pool under normal load and driving p99 latency from 180ms to 9.2s.

confidence.rationale: The reverted code passes the pool-exhaustion test that fails on current; the metric inflection lines up with the deploy to within two minutes; no other change in the window touches the pool.

timeline.label: payments_pool_inuse saturates at max_size=10; wait queue begins growing

evidence.detail: PR #482 'tune pool sizing' changed POOL_MAX_SIZE from 50 to 10 in demo-app/payments/pool.py; merged 01:58, only change in the 30m pre-alert window touching payments.
payments_pool_inuse pinned at 10 (==max) from 02:11; payments_pool_wait_seconds p99 climbs in lockstep with request latency.

acquire() in demo-app/payments/pool.py blocks on an asyncio.Semaphore sized by POOL_MAX_SIZE; the checkout path holds a connection across an external call, so a small pool serialises all in-flight payments.
test_pool_exhaustion asserts p95 acquire latency < 200ms under 25 concurrent callers; fails on current (max_size=10), passes on revert (max_size=50).

code_path.summary: Inbound charge -> ConnectionPool.acquire() blocks on an undersized semaphore -> request latency tracks pool wait time.

nodes.note: Entry point; acquires a pooled connection before writing the ledger row.
Blocks on asyncio.Semaphore(POOL_MAX_SIZE); POOL_MAX_SIZE was cut to 10 in PR #482.
Reads POOL_MAX_SIZE; this is the line the regression changed.

supporting_telemetry.observation: Flatlines at 10 from 02:11, i.e. the pool is fully saturated.
p99 rises from 0.18s to 9.2s starting 02:11, two minutes after the deploy.

blast_radius.note: All four depend on payments (ledger transitively via refunds). A full rollback of PR #482 reverts unrelated pool-metrics instrumentation also in that PR, so it is not a clean revert.

recommended_action.reasoning: A one-line forward-fix (restore POOL_MAX_SIZE=50, keep the new metrics) is safer than reverting the whole PR. Roll it to one payments replica first, confirm payments_pool_inuse stops saturating, then complete. A blunt rollback would also undo the pool instrumentation that made this diagnosable.
